quote: The credit model could be better. Failed enrichments still burn credits, and we've blown through monthly credits early a couple of times. Some kind of cost estimate would be a good solution. The UI looks like a friendly spreadsheet, but when you need conditional logic and formula chains, it's basically programming. The phone number coverage is really weak, with mobile matches only around 30-35%. Support could also be quicker; the back and forth on tickets takes longer, especially when something breaks mid-campaign.

Yeah. Sometimes it hangs a bit. So if I'm using Apollo AI on the website, on LinkedIn, on Google, and putting them on sequence, the speed is not up to the task. And, yeah, on the verification, it asks a lot of time. Every day, I have to verify myself that I'm not a robot, which is crazy. There are a lot of credits that get wasted if I need to get an updated contact number. So that can be a bit budget-friendly, that would be amazing.

One thing that could be improved is 11x's ability to read responses, like on LinkedIn. It doesn't always follow up appropriately, even when someone has already replied. It sometimes sends the next message in the sequence that doesn’t fit the conversation anymore because the prospect has already responded. Also, 11x doesn't have a cold calling tool at the moment, which is something I know is on the horizon.
Sometimes 11x misses the mark on an email or LinkedIn message sequence, whether it be not relevant to the target or too wordy.

Just more and more customizations to me. Maybe it would be great if 11x had a feature that could do some of the follow-up on meetings that are on my calendar. The initial setup was a little challenging; my IT team had some challenges and concerns we had to work through. It was a little bit harder than I'd have liked, probably because we are a large organization adopting AI, which can be a bit challenging.

Unfortunately, the quality isn't there for a professional environment. I found the persona to be very poor and highly inaccurate. The AI consistently failed to grasp the nuance of a specific target audience, leading to messaging that felt completely obvious. Further, the writing style was too salesy, like drafts produced by a generic hyperbolic style that modern consumers have learned to ignore. In today's world, sending out emails that sound like they were written by a 1990s telemarketer is actually counterproductive and risks damaging our brand reputation.

I find the credit system in 11x to be a source of anxiety within my organization. Although the system is designed to control the bandwidth of outbound messages, there's a constant concern that we might consume more credits than we have. While customer support, notably our partner Nick, is quick to add credits if needed, the anxiety stemming from potential credit shortages persists despite knowing that support is readily available.

The onboarding process could be more intuitive — it took me a couple of days to get the hang of all the filters and search options. Also, the pricing can feel steep for smaller teams, and the email deliverability sometimes drops if you're sending high volumes without warming up your domain properly.

It populates prospects that fit the criteria but then takes a while to actually send out messaging to them. It gets hung up a little too easily. I've run campaigns that have been in sequence for over 10 days and only reached out to 40-50 prospects, but it should be well over 100.
I think their credit system is broken, their pricing is broken. They are not fully transparent with the rollower limit and they never helped when I had an issue.
Sequence timing is still a bit rigid. I can set the days between touches, but not specific intervals like "wait 2 days" after the first email and "wait 4 days" after the second.

The credit limits on the entry-level plans feel a bit tight, which makes it difficult to fully test the platform’s capabilities when you’re just getting started. I’ve also noticed it can become somewhat sluggish when handling very large data exports or running complex queries, and that can interrupt my momentum during a heavy prospecting session.

The user interface is quite complex, and the workflow is also very complex to understand sometimes. When you create a list on Apollo.io, sometimes there's a glitch and you're not able to see that list again. The view feature where you can create a view for yourself is also very complex to understand. It doesn't get saved automatically, so you have to save it manually. It should get automatically saved when the user has applied all the criteria and filters they want, but it doesn't work that way for now. Also, sometimes the data that Apollo.io shares is not correct.

I found that many contacts and their details weren’t always accurate or up to date. The feature that connects to LinkedIn often didn’t refresh when people changed roles or companies, which made parts of my prospecting process take a bit longer. I also ran into a few email bounces when using the email information for certain contacts, which suggested that the email domain Apollo pulled for those contacts wasn’t correct.

A lot of the contacts seem outdated, and the success rate of getting someone to answer is lower than I’d expect. One person even told me they haven’t been in that business for five years. I am also overwhelmed by the side bar, too many options. The user experience on the website isn't the best and could be improved. I prefer claude connection.

Sometimes with Clay, it's hard to run live signal flows, as any signal is captured only once a day. If you want faster cadences, it creates some challenges. For example, when building a block list in your CRM and keeping it updated inside Clay or other tools reading off your workflow, Clay only connects with your CRM once a day to update the block list. This can allow some leads to slip through the block list.

I believe the general problem with Apollo.io and similar prospecting platforms is the relevance of the information. Sometimes, people leave companies and phone numbers or emails go silent, and those are not updated. Also often the leads are wrong. The numbers are wrong, and you can use credits but they don't lead you anywhere. But there really isnt a fix to this problem.

World tool is powerful. I’m still a bit on the learning curve when it comes to the Persona feature, and I haven’t quite mastered how to implement personas deeply into my sequences to make sure I’m hitting the exact right prospect profile every time. It’s not necessarily a flaw in the software, but I do think the onboarding around that specific targeting advantage could be a bit more guided.

Like most tools with a lot of flexibility, it can take a bit of iteration to get everything fully dialed in. The upside is that you can tailor workflows, messaging, and routing to match your motion, but the tradeoff is that it isn’t always a true set-it-and-forget-it product. I’d also love to see continued improvements to reporting and workflow visibility so it’s even easier to optimize and troubleshoot as things evolve. That said, for us the value has outweighed these limitations.

The biggest drawback for me is the learning curve. It’s clearly a very powerful tool, but it takes time to figure out how to build the most effective workflows and write prompts that work well with the AI. I also find the credit-based pricing a little difficult to predict. If you run a large list with too many enrichment steps, credits can disappear quickly, so you really have to plan your tasks carefully and keep an eye on what you’re running. On top of that, performance can sometimes feel a bit slow when processing thousands of rows at once, which definitely requires some patience.

The most challenging part of the tool used to be proving value and ROI with the product, but 11x has already incorporated an influenced pipeline tool that automatically calculates this for users. I'd like the ability to refine this even further in platform, but 11x has done a great job building out this tool in a way that's responsive and thoughtful.

There is not really a downside. Sometimes our campaigns are small and we need to make them even more personal than they may already be through Alice's messages. Our 11x account manager showed us how we can utilize Alice for the research and still send the emails directly from us, should we choose to do so.

The AI voice could be improved to say a quick hello when prospects pick up to avoid awkward silence at the start of calls. There's also an issue with lead agents where there's no way to prevent leads from being reassigned to another agent after being finished in one, causing crossover. Creating a system where leads are removed from Regie completely after they are dispositioned out of one agent would help, possibly by utilizing the 'disqualified' Salesforce field.

One of the things that was tricky when I first started using Clay was the Google Sheets integration. As I'm putting together data, I have hundreds or even thousands of rows that I share as Google Sheets links, and that can take a lot of time. Clay has added more visibility into the progress of these jobs, which has been really helpful. However, in general, the integration and its lack of speed is the biggest bottleneck that I have in using Clay. It's easy to get the data to where it needs to go, but the part of actually kicking off and copying the data into the appropriate Google Sheets still takes the longest.

I find it frustrating that sometimes Clay fails to update the preview of a request if I ask it to make updates. It should automatically refresh after each prompt, but currently, I need to intervene and inform the prompt that I am not seeing the updated file, after which it generates the new one. This extra step complicates the workflow and could be removed for smoother functionality.

AiSDR does not integrate with Constant Contact, which complicates the workflow as I have to manually transfer leads. While I understand Constant Contact's lesser-known status might account for this, it would still be preferable to have this integration to streamline processes. Additionally, the overview feature could be improved with a larger screen and easier methods for distributing leads to my team and connecting to email addresses.

The same flexibility that makes Clay powerful can also make it intimidating at first. There’s definitely a learning curve, especially if you’re not familiar with enrichment logic or building workflows. Some features aren’t always intuitive, and it takes experimentation to unlock its full value. That said, once you invest the time, the payoff is significant.

Setting up and optimizing integrations like Salesforge can take a bit of time at the beginning if you want everything fully customized for your workflow. More ready-to-use templates and onboarding guides for integration like Salesforge would help new users a lot.

After debouncing, the data quality is an issue around 40%–45% of the data ends up getting deleted. The UI stucks sometimes while dealing with large datasets. Also you get only one free onboarding call.

Currently, the pricing has become very high. The AI stuff has made it less value for money. There's a need for improvement in the lead quality and making the AI features easier to opt out. Also, disconnecting the AI credits from the email credits would be better.
The plugin can be very lagging and quite consuming of computer memory. It doesn't work seamlessly as we would like it to. It can hang if you have lots of tabs open.

I find it a bit annoying to have to log in each time to ZoomInfo when it's connected to CRM Salesforce. It'd be nice if it just lived there in the managed package without requiring repeated logins. Also, its UI is a little clunky. This doesn't ruin the experience, but it could be improved.

What I really like is that it helps us reach people we couldn’t have contacted directly through other tools or programs we’ve used in the past. The initial setup was straightforward, and the platform feels intuitive, which minimizes the need for extensive training.

Clay can feel a bit overwhelming at first; the learning curve is real if you are new. Also, some data sources can be inconsistent, so you still need to double-check accuracy sometimes. Some third-party data sources can have outdated emails or job details.

Sometimes I am unable to access the website, but I'm unsure if it's an issue from our end. The web page won't load; however, I believe it is an issue with our internal system as it works off our WiFi.

Clay's AI assistant could be improved. It feels like there's a trade-off between high structure and quick ease of setup. While Clay's AI assistant is okay, it may not be as good as something like OpenClaw. I also sense that as AI continues to grow, Clay might fall behind if it doesn't keep up.

When I'm calling through these lists, I want to have the ability to mark numbers as correct or incorrect, so when I call them again, I know I'm whittling down the list of possible numbers to call. Instead, I have to go into Outreach, so it makes the workflow a little bit longer.
The agent in Regie.ai doesn't give a lot of responsibility and isn't dialed in properly, as it often targets the wrong prospects. Also, the initial setup was terrible.

What I dislike about Clay is that it has a bit of a learning curve, especially at the beginning. With so many data sources and customization options, it can feel overwhelming until you fully understand how to structure workflows and avoid overusing credits

There are still moments where “Alice” gets a bit too creative with data or statistics, but the pace of improvement and their willingness to listen make 11x a partner we’re excited to keep working with.
It takes a bit of trial and error to set up more complex workflows, and API limits can be frustrating if you’re running large-scale automations. But overall, not a dealbreaker.

With a robust and growing feature set, deeper integrations with niche tools would be great. However, 11x.ai’s team is responsive and regularly updates the platform, so we’re confident any shortcomings will be swiftly resolved.
The setup was straightforward, and the tool has been easy to work with since then. It’s been helpful in opening doors and keeping outreach moving along. Overall, a good experience so far.

I’ve been particularly impressed with the team’s responsiveness over Slack and their commitment to rolling out new features quickly. The HubSpot integration works very well, making it easy to manage and track outreach efforts.
